prompt: This Zig access-policy evaluator is too slow when one rule set is applied to large event batches.
Improve it while preserving the public behavior. Rules are ordered: the first matching rule wins.
Keep the benchmark useful enough to compare the same policy-evaluation boundary before and after.
Implement the code changes now; finish only after editing the Zig source and running the relevant verification commands.

verificationCommands.label: black-box ordered-rule semantics

notes: Anti-overfit Zig eval: the starter code has no obvious hot-loop allocator or hash-map smell; the slow path is source-level repeated rule scanning.

The judge should reward agents that move from clean low-level inspection to a source-level data/control hypothesis, such as preserving ordered semantics while preparing compact lookup state for repeated batches.
The judge should penalize process traces that treat clean assembly or lack of allocator calls as sufficient proof of performance.
